summary: Iteration 4 testing — (1) Tawk.to live-chat widget fully removed and (2) AI chat file/image attachments. All 13 backend tests PASS (upload, fetch, reject paths, attach-message + admin visibility). Frontend E2E passes: Tawk has 0 network hits on Landing+Status, no Tawk_API in window, AIWidget paperclip opens file picker, pending preview renders, remove-file works, send-with-only-files enabled, bubble-image renders inline, assistant ack appears within ~5s via polling, and 4-file cap is enforced with a 'MAX 4 FILES AT ONCE.' warning when 5 files are attempted.

minor.issue: Missing session_id returns 422 (FastAPI Form validation) instead of the 400 implied by the spec. Acceptable — behavior documented and handled in tests.

Mismatched session with valid file_ids returns 400 only when ALL ids are unknown to the session. If caller mixes one valid + one foreign id, the foreign one is silently dropped (message still succeeds). Not a security issue because file access is still admin-scoped, but worth logging a warning.

issues: The small '⚠ MAX 4 FILES AT ONCE.' warning uses low-contrast yellow on a semi-transparent background. Readable but borderline on bright displays.

action_items: (Optional) Normalize /api/ai/upload missing session_id to 400 for spec consistency (currently 422).
(Optional) Log a warning when /api/ai/attach-message receives a mix of valid + foreign file_ids instead of silently dropping.
critical_code_review_comments: auth_and_chat.py: _safe_ext correctly blocks path-traversal extensions and falls back to mimetypes.guess_extension — good.
auth_and_chat.py: ai_upload reads MAX_UPLOAD_BYTES+1 in memory then checks length — fine for 8MB cap but note this loads the full file in RAM; for larger caps in future, switch to streaming write.
auth_and_chat.py: ai_get_upload uses a strict hex regex for file_id — prevents path traversal via the URL. Good.
AIWidget.jsx: send() switches to /ai/attach-message when files present AND skips LLM, which matches the spec. Polling picks up the assistant ack (~3-5s). Good.

Admin.jsx lines 1934, 1953, 1970: inbox-image-{id} / inbox-file-{id} testids render from m.attachments — matches backend contract verified in test_iteration_4_attachments.py::test_attach_message_persists_and_acks.
seed_data_creation: Created ~3 TEST_sess_* ai_sessions and ~4 ai_uploads entries (small PNGs + a stub PDF) during the attach-message test. All files live at /app/backend/uploads/ai_chat/ and are 8x8 px / <100 B each — safe to leave or purge.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest backend/tests/test_iteration_4_attachments.py -v` from /app for regression. Frontend AIWidget launcher is data-testid='ai-fab' (NOT 'ai-widget-launcher'). File input is data-testid='ai-widget-file-input' and has the `multiple` attribute, so you can set_input_files([path]*n) to test the 4-file cap — the widget replaces (not appends) when set_input_files overrides the selection, but handleFileSelect still enforces MAX_FILES=4 and shows a yellow warning pill. Assistant ack text contains 'Got your file' and appears via /ai/poll within ~5s. Tawk is fully gone: 0 network requests and no window.Tawk_API on Landing or /status/*. Admin inbox attachments rendering was NOT end-to-end browser-verified (admin login page hit a navigation timeout once) but the JSX rendering the inbox-image-{id} / inbox-file-{id} testids is present at Admin.jsx:1953 and Admin.jsx:1970, and the backend endpoint /api/ai/admin/sessions/{sid}/messages correctly returns attachments[] (verified in test_attach_message_persists_and_acks).
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found. Minor spec deviations noted above are by-design FastAPI behaviors (422 for missing Form field) or defensive no-ops (silent drop of foreign ids when at least one valid id is present).
